title: Rich Double Polish Custard Galette
description: Sweet and crispy Polish dessert made with custard, scored fat, and fresh berries. This vegan and gluten-free galette is perfect for meal prep and Buddhist temple-friendly gatherings.

ingredients.name: Non-dairy milk
ingredients.notes: such as almond or soy milk

instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, sugar, and custard powder.
Add the cold unsalted vegan butter and use a pastry blender or your fingers to work it into the flour mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually pour in the non-dairy milk, stirring with a fork until the dough comes together in a ball.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and roll it out to a thickness of about 1/8 inch (3 mm).
Transfer the dough to a 9-inch (23 cm) tart pan with a removable bottom.
In a separate bowl, mix the blueberries with 1 tablespoon of confectioners' sugar (if using).
Arrange the blueberries on top of the dough, leaving a 1-inch (2.5 cm) border around the edges.
Fold the edges of the dough up over the filling, pressing gently to seal.
Bake the galette for 35-40 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the filling is bubbly.
Remove the galette from the oven and let it cool on a wire rack for at least 10 minutes before serving.
